subject: comfy-kitchen is BENIGN — guilt by association with the OOM. Confirmed from app.log. Drop it from the v0.26 plan; keep only a 'verify clean' check.
body:
Answering your 5 questions. Your hypothesis is correct: comfy-kitchen never failed; it got blamed because the [INFO] probe line was the only log text visible when the user screenshotted the OOM dialog (MPI-144). The user CALLED it 'three errors' but that was three repeated INFO capability-probe lines (one per ComfyUI restart), not three errors.

IMPORTANT SOURCE CAVEAT: I only ever saw the SINGLE comfy-kitchen line the user pasted from the remote Pod container log — I never had the full remote container log scrolled. BUT the app captures the LOCAL ComfyUI startup in logs/app.log, and comfy-kitchen logs IDENTICALLY there (same backend, same version 0.2.10 pinned, same probe). So I can answer authoritatively from the local log + the one pasted remote line (which is byte-identical in shape to the local INFO lines).

1. ACTUAL error/traceback? NO. Every comfy-kitchen line across ~6 ComfyUI startups in app.log is [INFO], available:True, disabled:False, unavailable_reason:None. No traceback, no ERROR, nowhere. The pasted remote line is the same: available:True / disabled:False / unavailable_reason:None = loading CLEANLY. comfy-kitchen got blamed by association for a benign probe. Confirmed.

2. Real failure? NONE. The OOM that session was the VRAM OOM (normalvram on a 24GB 4090 keeping the 42GB LTX transformer resident) — fixed by MPI-144 (--lowvram, image v0.8.1, verified). comfy-kitchen had nothing to do with it; the probe just happened to be on screen.

3. fp8/nvfp4 quantized path erroring downstream? NO error seen. app.log:1312/1331 reports: 'Native ops: float8_e4m3fn, float8_e5m2 , emulated ops: mxfp8, nvfp4'. So fp8 (e4m3fn/e5m2) is a NATIVE hw path; mxfp8 + nvfp4 are EMULATED. That is a capability report, not an attempt-and-fail. No 'unsupported' / 'not implemented for' / dtype-fallback line anywhere near a gen. NOTE: the workflow's LTX weights are bf16 (filenames _bf16) + the gemma encoder is fp8 (gemma-3-12b-it-heretic-fp8) — comfy-kitchen's fp8 native ops likely service the gemma fp8 path cleanly. No quant error.

4. The ONLY 'unavailable' backend is TRITON: available:False, disabled:True, unavailable_reason: "ImportError: No module named 'triton'". That is benign — eager + cuda backends are both available:True and serve the ops; triton is optional. (Tangent worth noting for MPI-145: no triton also means a sage-attention compile path may be affected — sage uses triton — but that is the sage card's problem, not comfy-kitchen.)

5. Card/profile/version: the app.log lines are the LOCAL engine (the user's box). The pasted comfy-kitchen line was from a remote Pod — per the surrounding session it was the cu124 4090 OOM run on v0.8.1 (the OOM screenshot). comfy-kitchen 0.2.10 is identical local + remote (core pin), so profile doesn't change the verdict.

CARRY INTO THE v0.26 BUMP VERIFY (the real risks, NOT comfy-kitchen):
- node-pin compat: re-verify all 8 baked nodes import on v0.26 (KJNodes/Impact/LTXVideo/MpiNodes/etc). LTXVideo's low_vram_loaders + the LatentUpscaleModelLoader folder-type (latent_upscale_models) must still work — that was the MPI-143 trap.
- kornia: keep the kornia==0.8.2 pin (LTXVideo pyramid.pad) unless v0.26's bundled LTXVideo path changed — re-check the Dockerfile import-check still passes.
- sage vs SDPA: all remote gens ran SDPA (sage never installed — MPI-145). triton ImportError above is consistent with that. Don't assume sage on v0.26 without re-testing the bootstrap.
- memory: --lowvram (MPI-144) + --cache-ram (MPI-142) are the working memory config; carry both into v0.26. Per-card VRAM is MPI-146.

VERDICT: comfy-kitchen is a NON-ISSUE. Drop it from the v0.26 plan; keep only a one-line 'confirm comfy-kitchen still probes clean (available:True) on the v0.26 floor' smoke check. If you want the actual REMOTE container log to triple-confirm, ask the user to paste RunPod -> Logs -> Container from a v0.8.1 gen (got prompt -> Prompt executed) — but I'm confident it's clean. Reply if you need the local app.log line numbers (146-148, 754-756, 1169-1171, 1312, 1331).

recent_events.outcome: Answer applied to plan: comfy-kitchen dropped to a smoke check; triton-missing lead added to MPI-145.
